userStory: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials, so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
applicationContext: WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
entryConditions: Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials, so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at the exact URL:
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
Successful authentication returns the user to `home_page` in authenticated state.
behaviorRules: The Login page must be opened through the `home_page` person/profile button for this workflow.
Password input must be masked by default.
Valid credentials must authenticate the user successfully.
Invalid credentials must not authenticate the user.
Authentication failures, including blank username, blank password, partial credentials, and incorrect credentials, must display only the generic `Failed` message.
Separate required-field validation messaging must not be displayed.
Failed authentication must maintain unauthenticated state.
Leading and trailing whitespace handling must follow implemented application behavior and be observationally validated.
Back and Home buttons on the Login page must return the user to `home_page` without authentication.
Successful authentication must return the user to `home_page` in authenticated state.
validationExpectations: The Login page opens successfully from `home_page`.
Username, Password, and Login controls are visible and interactive.
Back and Home buttons are visible and functional.
Password input remains masked during entry.
Invalid credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
Blank credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
Partial credential submissions display the generic `Failed` message.
No dedicated required-field validation message is displayed.
Failed authentication does not transition the application into authenticated state.
Successful authentication using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated `home_page`.
Authenticated user identity and authenticated-only features are visible after successful login.
Whitespace-modified credential handling is validated according to implemented application behavior.
transitionExpectations: Clicking the person/profile button transitions the application from `home_page` in `guest` state to the Login page.
Successful authentication transitions the application from the Login page to `home_page` in authenticated state.
Failed authentication keeps the user on the Login page or otherwise maintains unauthenticated state.
Invalid, blank, partial, and whitespace-modified credential failures must not transition the application into authenticated state.
Back button navigation returns the user from the Login page to `home_page` in unauthenticated state.
Home button navigation returns the user from the Login page to `home_page` in unauthenticated state.
Independent negative and return-navigation branches must begin from the canonical `home_page` `guest` entry context.
The unauthenticated entry state must be restored before executing each independent branch.
Transition from `home_page` to the Login page.
Validate Login page controls and behaviors.
Submit authentication using the Login button.
reuseGuidance: Reuse the existing Login page resource for:

Verify Person Profile Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Back Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Notification Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Home Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Customer Signup Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Clean Survey Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Site Survey Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Navigation To Customer Search Page
Verify Navigation To Vehicle Survey Page
Verify Navigation To Site Survey Page
Wait For Element To Be Ready

keywords: Verify Home Page Loaded In Guest State
Verify Person Profile Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Back Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Notification Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Home Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Customer Signup Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Clean Survey Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Site Survey Button Visible And Enabled
Verify Navigation To Customer Search Page
Verify Navigation To Vehicle Survey Page
Verify Navigation To Site Survey Page

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest user can open the Login page from home_page using the person/profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from home_page guest state to the Login page and the browser URL matches the approved login navigation pattern /washtabui/login?data=undefined.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page controls are visible and interactable after navigation from home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible, enabled, and available for user interaction on the Login page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password input remains masked during credential entry
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Password field masks entered characters during input and does not display the plain text password value.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify successful authentication with approved credentials returns the user to authenticated home_page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application authenticates the user successfully, returns to home_page in authenticated state, displays authenticated user identity information, and displays authenticated-only functionality.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username and invalid password display only the generic Failed authentication message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message is shown, and the application remains unauthenticated on the Login page or in an unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify blank username and blank password submission displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated without transition to authenticated home_page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify login attempt with populated username and blank password displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the application remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify login attempt with blank username and populated password displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the application remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify login with valid username and incorrect password does not authenticate the user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, authenticated-only UI is not shown, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify login with incorrect username and valid password does not authenticate the user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, authenticated identity information is not displayed, and the application remains in unauthenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify leading whitespace in username credential is handled according to implemented application behavior
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application processes the whitespace-modified username according to implemented behavior and either authenticates successfully or displays only the generic Failed message while preserving an observable authenticated or unauthenticated state transition outcome.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify trailing whitespace in password credential is handled according to implemented application behavior
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application processes the whitespace-modified password according to implemented behavior and either authenticates successfully or displays only the generic Failed message while preserving observable authentication state behavior.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify whitespace-only credentials do not authenticate the user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button returns the user from Login page to home_page in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only UI is not displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button returns the user from Login page to home_page in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only features are not visible.

validations: The application transitions from home_page guest state to the Login page and the browser URL matches the approved login navigation pattern /washtabui/login?data=undefined.
The Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible, enabled, and available for user interaction on the Login page.
The Password field masks entered characters during input and does not display the plain text password value.
The application authenticates the user successfully, returns to home_page in authenticated state, displays authenticated user identity information, and displays authenticated-only functionality.

Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message is shown, and the application remains unauthenticated on the Login page or in an unauthenticated state.

Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated without transition to authenticated home_page.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the application remains unauthenticated.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, authenticated-only UI is not shown, and the user remains unauthenticated.
Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, authenticated identity information is not displayed, and the application remains in unauthenticated state.

The application processes the whitespace-modified username according to implemented behavior and either authenticates successfully or displays only the generic Failed message while preserving an observable authenticated or unauthenticated state transition outcome.

The application processes the whitespace-modified password according to implemented behavior and either authenticates successfully or displays only the generic Failed message while preserving observable authentication state behavior.
Authentication fails, only the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.
The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only UI is not displayed.
The application returns the user to home_page in unauthenticated guest state and authenticated-only features are not visible.

AUT-WT-LOGIN05: Verify invalid username and invalid password display only the generic Failed authentication message
AUT-WT-LOGIN06: Verify blank username and blank password submission displays only the generic Failed message
AUT-WT-LOGIN07: Verify login attempt with populated username and blank password displays only the generic Failed message
AUT-WT-LOGIN08: Verify login attempt with blank username and populated password displays only the generic Failed message
AUT-WT-LOGIN09: Verify login with valid username and incorrect password does not authenticate the user
AUT-WT-LOGIN10: Verify login with incorrect username and valid password does not authenticate the user
AUT-WT-LOGIN11: Verify leading whitespace in username credential is handled according to implemented application behavior
AUT-WT-LOGIN12: Verify trailing whitespace in password credential is handled according to implemented application behavior

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.

unresolvedGaps: The login username input field required for authentication workflows is missing from the approved elements.
The password input field required for authentication and password masking validation is missing from the approved elements.
The primary login submission control is missing from the approved page model.
The generic Failed authentication message expected by all negative authentication scenarios is missing.
The artifact does not provide observable negative-outcome validation support for failed authentication scenarios beyond remaining on the page.
The person/profile button belongs to the upstream shared home_page workflow context and should not be owned by login_page.
The notification button is unrelated to the documented login workflow and adds unnecessary page noise.
The customer signup button is unrelated to login authentication coverage and appears to belong to another business workflow.
The clean survey button is unrelated to the login workflow.
The site survey button is unrelated to the login workflow.
The locator xpath=//a is dangerously generic and likely to match multiple links across the application.
This generic anchor element duplicates the Home button behavior already represented by auto_home_btn_button.
The background content container is a decorative layout wrapper and not meaningful for workflow automation.
The approved element naming relies on implementation IDs instead of business semantics.
The approved element naming is implementation-centric rather than workflow-centric.
